items.title: Warren Buffett Will Give Away His Entire $140 Billion Berkshire Stake by 2034
items.summary: Warren Buffett announced he will donate his entire $140 billion Berkshire Hathaway stake to charity by December 31, 2034. He recently donated $5.96 billion worth of shares and still owns approximately $140.25 billion in Class A shares. The article notes this philanthropic move should not significantly impact Berkshire's stock price, as it represents a transfer rather than a sale, and shareholders should focus on the company's long-term performance under new CEO Greg Abel.

items.title: Some of Berkshire's Newest Bets Aren't American. Greg Abel Bought 3 Japanese Trading Houses Last Quarter.
items.summary: Under new CEO Greg Abel, Berkshire Hathaway has increased its stakes in three Japanese trading houses—Mitsubishi, Sumitomo, and Marubeni—during Q2 2026. Berkshire's combined five trading house positions, worth $35.4 billion (up from $23.5 billion a year prior), generate $862 million in annual dividends and are funded with cheap yen-denominated debt at 1.2% interest, creating an attractive 5.6% yield on original cost.

items.title: Prediction: This Magnificent Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock Will Become Berkshire's Next Forever Holding
items.summary: Berkshire Hathaway has significantly increased its stake in Alphabet, tripling its position from 17.8 million shares in Q3 2025 to 54 million shares by Q1 2026, and recently committed an additional $10 billion through a private placement. The article argues that Alphabet exhibits characteristics of Berkshire's 'forever stocks' due to its diverse business model, dominant search monopoly, high-margin cloud services, and shareholder-friendly capital allocation, positioning it as a long-term compounder similar to Coca-Cola.
